explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in B tier with an overall score of 27 from a base nutrition score of 15. Context lifted it by 12 points.

topPros.title: simple traditional fat
topPros.explanation: This gives butter a cleaner identity than heavily processed fat substitutes.

topPros.title: very strong culinary usefulness
topPros.explanation: Butter has real kitchen legitimacy and practical use that matters in this category.

topPros.title: can fit as a moderate traditional fat
topPros.explanation: The editorial position here is that butter belongs as a flawed but legitimate middle-ground fat rather than a bottom-tier industrial product.

topCons.title: saturated fat is a major drawback
topCons.explanation: This is the main reason butter cannot be an elite oils-and-fats food.

topCons.title: cholesterol is high
topCons.explanation: A second meaningful strike against the fat profile.

topCons.title: fat profile is clearly weaker than top-tier oils
topCons.explanation: Butter is not competing with the best oils on chemistry alone.
